steps.title: Find the grammatical subject
steps.content: The subject is “each one of a ghazal's couplets,” where “each one” is the head noun phrase. The prepositional phrase “of a ghazal's couplets” does not change the subject's number—it remains singular.

steps.title: Match the verb to the subject's number
steps.content: Subject-verb agreement requires a singular subject to take a singular verb. “Is” is singular. The other choices are non-singular forms that violate this rule. That confirms A is correct.
